title: Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
title_candidates: Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
Why Podcast Production Breaks Even When the Recording Is Great
From Raw Recording to Publish: The Systems Behind a Great Podcast
title_approved: Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
description_short: Maya Chen breaks down the five stages of a reliable podcast production pipeline and explains why the pipeline — not just the conversation — is the product.
description_long: Most podcast teams think the product is the conversation. Maya Chen argues the pipeline is the product. In this episode, she walks through the five stages of a reliable production workflow — capture, post-production, content extraction, review and approval, and distribution — and explains why small teams break down at handoffs, inconsistent naming, and the false assumption that a raw transcript equals publish-ready content. Practical guidance for two- and three-person teams on folder structure, output bundles, automation limits, title strategy, and the five highest-value pre-publish checks.

sponsor.name: SoundLayer
sponsor.description: Collaboration tool for small media teams that helps organize transcripts, asset versions, and review workflows.

chapters.label: What to extract vs. what needs review

quote_pull: The transcript is a rich input, not the final source of editorial truth. — Maya Chen
